updates.tweet_text: There's no shortcut to screen time. Profitability requires 1) Finding a setup that has an actual edge and embedding it in a trade management process and 2) Thousands upon thousands of reps of that setup. Seeing it in every context, every variation, every volatility regime
updates.notes: Tweet is general trading philosophy/advice with no prices, levels, or actionable setups mentioned whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text: @Pete66668332642 Yes market loves to do one thing for weeks or days, then totally switch it up!
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about market behavior; no price levels named or actionable triggers of any kind.

updates.tweet_text: @Trevormiles16 Gap down into Failed Breakdown as always on Sundays! (when we get a gap down that is)
updates.notes: Reply tweet with general commentary about Sunday gap-down FBD pattern; no specific price level named or actionable trigger identified.

updates.tweet_text: @EStrader33 @Trevormiles16 I missed that one as it triggered on Ffriday 130AM and I don't trade overnight. Worked nicely though for those who took it! The fresh low wouldve been Friday's daily low and that played out very nicely
updates.notes: Conversational reply about a missed overnight trade; no actionable price named, just hindsight observation of Friday's daily low playing out.

updates.tweet_text: @fiveinchhammer @EStrader33 @Trevormiles16 That one was very risky since the zone was already traded though and triggered on Friday morning (we already got that long) and there was a fresh low on Friday's daily low that would serve as a magnet. That one worked though as we got to 43 1st up still and that low was 33
updates.notes: Conversational reply referencing past trades and existing morning-plan levels (7243, 7233 area); no new actionable levels named with qualifying language.

updates.tweet_text:
Last eve, #ES_F got same trade we've seen hundreds of times newsletter readers know well: Gap down into Failed Breakdown. We swept Friday's 7520 low, got long. Targets 7535 (hit), ~7559 (hit), 7570  (hit)

Plan: Ride your runner. 7590, 7600, 7611 next. 7535=support. Watch traps https://t.co/vtkJFEhnw1
updates.notes: All levels mentioned (7535, 7559, 7570, 7590, 7611) are morning-plan reaffirmations or hit-target observations; 7600 is conversational fill between plan levels. No new actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text: @ES_Money_Print I missed the 35 failed breakdown because it was at 1:30AM on Friday morning. At that point the fresh low was at 7521 Friday low but I obviously couldn't write about that one since it formed after my last newsletter on Thursday came out due to the holiday. I traded the fresh low.
updates.notes: Reply explaining missed newsletter timing; 7521 is a hindsight trade reference, not a new actionable level. No naming language used.

updates.tweet_text: @ES_Money_Print (and it actually popped to 43 2x so there would've been two chances to take profit there)
updates.notes: Casual observation about price popping to 43 (likely ~7543); no naming language, no actionable level, pure hindsight trade commentary.

updates.tweet_text:
Last night we got the standard Sunday gap down into Failed Breakdown in #ES_F (my absolute favorite trade). We swept Friday's 7521 low to 7514, recovered, ripped +50.

Now, nothing to do. 7590, 7600, 7611 if ES wants more. No good supports until 7535 now (watch traps there) https://t.co/NXg3StXBks
updates.notes: 7600 is conversational upside reference; 7590, 7611, 7535 all in morning plan. 7521/7514 are observations of price action swept. No new actionable levels named.
